title: Sample Size Calculator (Industrial Engineering)
description: Determines the minimum sample size for process capability studies, audit sampling, and hypothesis testing based on ISO 2859, Six Sigma, and Lean standards.

inputs.label: Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Standard confidence levels per ISO 2859-1 and Six Sigma DMAIC.

inputs.label: Margin of Error (Precision)
inputs.businessContext: Acceptable deviation from true population parameter; typical for process audits.

inputs.label: Population Size (Lot Size)
inputs.businessContext: Total items in the lot or process output per period.

inputs.label: Expected Proportion (Defect Rate)
inputs.businessContext: Historical or assumed defect rate; used in attribute sampling.

inputs.label: Standard Deviation (Process Sigma)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated process variation for variables sampling (e.g., SPC).

inputs.label: Effect Size (Minimum Detectable Difference)
inputs.businessContext: Smallest practical change to detect in hypothesis testing (Six Sigma).

inputs.label: Sampling Method
inputs.businessContext: Design effect adjustment per WERC and ISO 2859 guidelines.

inputs.label: Apply Finite Population Correction
inputs.businessContext: When sample > 5% of population, correction reduces sample size.

rules.message: At 99% confidence, margin of error below 2% may require extremely large samples. Consider increasing margin or reducing confidence.
Expected proportion outside typical range (1-50%). Verify input or use variables sampling.
For small populations, a high margin of error may yield unreliable results. Consider census.

thresholds.alert: Sample size below 30 may violate normality assumptions. Use non-parametric methods or increase sample.
Sample size exceeds 50% of population. Consider full inspection (census) for cost efficiency.
Sample size > 5000 may incur significant inspection cost. Evaluate stratified sampling or reduced precision.

formulas.label: Z-Score from Confidence Level
formulas.description: Standard normal deviate corresponding to the selected confidence level.
formulas.formula: if confidenceLevel == '90' then 1.645 else if confidenceLevel == '95' then 1.96 else 2.576

formulas.label: Design Effect (DEFF)
formulas.description: Adjustment for sampling method; 1.0 for simple random, higher for cluster/stratified.
formulas.formula: if samplingMethod == 'simpleRandom' then 1.0 else if samplingMethod == 'stratified' then 0.9 else if samplingMethod == 'cluster' then 1.5 else 1.2

formulas.label: Sample Size (Infinite Population)
formulas.description: Initial sample size assuming infinite population (attribute sampling).

formulas.label: Sample Size (Finite Correction)
formulas.description: Adjusted sample size using finite population correction.
formulas.formula: if useFiniteCorrection then ceil(sampleSizeInfinite / (1 + (sampleSizeInfinite - 1) / populationSize)) else sampleSizeInfinite

formulas.label: Sample Size for Variables (Means)
formulas.description: Sample size for continuous data based on standard deviation and effect size.

formulas.label: Final Recommended Sample Size
formulas.description: Combined sample size considering both attribute and variables approaches, adjusted by design effect.

formulas.label: Data Confidence Adjusted
formulas.description: Confidence level adjusted for sample size adequacy relative to population.

primary.label: Minimum Required Sample Size
primary.description: The smallest sample size that meets the specified confidence, precision, and process assumptions.

breakdown.label: Calculation Breakdown
breakdown.description: Intermediate values used in the sample size determination.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Process Variation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Standard deviation > 2x effect size inflates sample size. Consider process improvement (SPC).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Very Low Expected Defect Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Proportion < 1% leads to extremely large samples. Use variables sampling or accept higher risk.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Cluster Sampling Inefficiency
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Design effect > 1.2 increases sample size. Consider stratified sampling to reduce cost.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Confidence Level
suggestedActions.description: If sample size is too large, consider 90% confidence instead of 95% or 99%.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Margin of Error
suggestedActions.description: Relaxing precision from 5% to 10% can dramatically reduce sample size.

suggestedActions.label: Switch to Stratified Sampling
suggestedActions.description: Stratified sampling often reduces sample size and improves representativeness.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Process Variation
suggestedActions.description: Invest in process control (SPC) to lower standard deviation and required sample size.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Effective confidence level after accounting for sample size relative to population.
